transcript.clean_text: welcome back to our 2021 jerid italia coverage here on gcn and eurosport today's stage was given a three-star difficulty rating with over 2 000 meters of climbing in total most of it located in the second half of the 189 kilometer stage starting in biella they head predominantly south towards the finish line in canale on their way there three categorized climbs but it's an unclassified one that could prove the most pivotal 2.6 k is at 7.1 percent and it comes with just 15 k's to go there was a bit of fight at the start of the stage two to get into the early breakaway eight riders eventually made the day's move in the pouring rain the best placed on gc being taco vanderhorn of intimacy wanted gobert he started the day in 79th position 52 seconds down on race leader philippe organa the first sprinter today came at the foot of the first time pillow continue where he left off last year taking maximum points for androni on to the first climb and it was bora handguard setting the pace as they had been for much of the day looking to give peter sagan his best opportunity of a stage win by pieing the pressure on the other sprinters and it was working yesterday stayed when a tim malia dropped with still k's to go into the final 20ks and the gap to the remainder of the early break down to just 40 seconds so we had fresh attacks from the pentaton judo tocconi following a move made by tony galapan of adja desert by the top of that final climb of the day they were just 19 seconds behind the two riders still out front polo and van der horn van der horn though wanted to give it one last roll of the dice and with pillow unable to follow the dutchman set off to try and do the impossible with under three k's to go somewhat unbelievably van der hall still had a margin of over 30 seconds on the main peloton 1k later it was down to 22 as tocconi and galapan were finally caught the peloton though had completely miscalculated the strength of van der horn in december he didn't even have a pro contract he was then picked up by intermarche wonti gobert and just five months later he'd taken his and their first grand tour stage win nobody could believe it not even the man himself behind him it was a bitterly disappointed david chimeli who would win the sprint but only for second place peters again unable to capitalise on the work for his team having to settle for it's going to take a long time for this to sink into taco vanderhorn but the dream is true you've just won a stage of the giroud italia chapeau this was the rest of the top ten elia viviani in fourth patrick bevan fifth making two riders from the israel startup nation in the top five vermeers gaviria and betjell rounding out the top eight in the mountains competition vincenzo albanese extended his lead but no change in the pink jersey either 90 years to the day since it was first awarded at the race it's philippo ghana who gets to spray the champagne on the podium with the feeney though amongst the riders dropped on the day it means that frost avenue almeida and cavania have all moved up moss goes up to six whilst vlasov is up to seventh on the menu tomorrow then even more climbing than today the giro never takes long to find some difficult terrain and that's exactly what they'll face on their route to sestela like today the first part is flat but the second half is anything but they finish just after the cole pascaroni 4.75 kilometers long 10 average gradient 16 max and it's just two and a half k's to the finish line from there they're sure to be fireworks and we hope to have your company for our live coverage on gcm plus or eurosport territory restrictions do apply though but we hope to see you tomorrow
